cards.title: Radiation-Hardened Microchips for Strategic Defense and Space Missions
body: The development of radiation-hardened microchips is crucial for strategic defense and space missions, where electronic components are exposed to harsh radiation environments. Recently, BAE Systems announced that its radiation-hardened microchip designed for strategic defense missions has passed a rigorous radiation test. This achievement demonstrates the microchip's ability to withstand the extreme conditions found in space and nuclear environments.

Radiation-hardened microchips are designed to resist the effects of ionizing radiation, which can cause damage to electronic components and lead to system failures. These microchips are fabricated using specialized processes and materials that provide increased resistance to radiation-induced damage. The use of radiation-hardened microchips is essential for ensuring the reliability and performance of electronic systems in space and defense applications.

The successful development and testing of radiation-hardened microchips have significant implications for the space and defense industries. As the number of space missions and satellite constellations continues to grow, the demand for reliable and radiation-resistant electronic components is increasing. The development of radiation-hardened microchips will enable the creation of more robust and resilient space-based systems, which is critical for ensuring the success of future space missions and strategic defense operations.
cards.why: The development of radiation-hardened microchips is crucial for the success of future space missions and strategic defense operations. As the space and defense industries continue to evolve, the demand for reliable and radiation-resistant electronic components will only increase. By investing in the development of radiation-hardened microchips, engineers and researchers can help ensure the creation of more robust and resilient space-based systems, which is essential for advancing our understanding of space and protecting national security interests.

sources.name: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine

cards.title: Synchronized Switching in Resonant Converters using Phase-Locked Loop (PLL) Control for High-Efficiency Power Electronics
body: Resonant converters, such as series-parallel resonant converters, have become increasingly popular in high-power applications due to their high efficiency and low electromagnetic interference (EMI). However, achieving synchronized switching in these converters can be challenging. One approach to address this issue is to use a phase-locked loop (PLL) control system. The PLL control system generates a clock signal that is synchronized with the resonant frequency of the converter, ensuring that the switching devices are turned on and off at the optimal times.

The PLL control system consists of a phase detector, a loop filter, and a voltage-controlled oscillator (VCO). The phase detector compares the phase of the resonant converter's output voltage with the phase of the clock signal and generates an error signal. The loop filter filters the error signal and generates a control signal that is used to adjust the frequency of the VCO. The VCO generates the clock signal that is used to control the switching devices. By using a PLL control system, the resonant converter can achieve high efficiency and low EMI, making it suitable for high-power applications such as electric vehicles and renewable energy systems.

The design of the PLL control system requires careful consideration of the converter's resonant frequency, the switching frequency, and the loop bandwidth. The resonant frequency of the converter is determined by the values of the inductors and capacitors in the resonant circuit. The switching frequency is determined by the clock signal generated by the VCO. The loop bandwidth determines the stability and the response time of the PLL control system. A higher loop bandwidth can provide faster response time but may also increase the noise sensitivity of the system.

The advantages of using a PLL control system in resonant converters include high efficiency, low EMI, and high reliability. The PLL control system can also be used to control multiple resonant converters in parallel, making it suitable for high-power applications. However, the design of the PLL control system can be complex and requires careful consideration of the converter's resonant frequency, the switching frequency, and the loop bandwidth.
cards.why: The ability to achieve high efficiency and low EMI in power electronic systems is crucial for many applications, including electric vehicles, renewable energy systems, and data centers. Synchronized switching in resonant converters using PLL control can help achieve these goals, making it an important area of research and development for engineers. By understanding how to design and implement PLL control systems, engineers can create more efficient and reliable power electronic systems that can meet the demands of modern technology.

cards.title: Russia Intensifies Attacks on Kyiv Ahead of NATO Summit
body: Deadly Russian strikes have hit Kyiv, the capital city of Ukraine, on the eve of a crucial NATO summit. The attacks come as tensions between Russia and Ukraine continue to escalate, with both sides engaged in a prolonged conflict.

The strikes, which have caused significant damage and loss of life, are seen as a strategic move by Russia to exert pressure on Ukraine and its Western allies. The timing of the attacks, just before the NATO summit, is particularly notable, as it highlights Russia's determination to disrupt the alliance's efforts to support Ukraine.

NATO's summit is expected to focus on the ongoing conflict in Ukraine, as well as the alliance's broader strategy for addressing security challenges in the region. The summit will bring together leaders from NATO member countries, including the United States, to discuss key issues and coordinate a unified response to the crisis.
cards.why: The recent Russian strikes on Kyiv are significant because they demonstrate the escalating nature of the conflict between Russia and Ukraine, and the potential consequences for global security. The attacks also highlight the importance of NATO's role in supporting Ukraine and maintaining stability in the region, making the upcoming summit a critical moment for international diplomacy and cooperation.

cards.title: The 1980 US Boycott of the Moscow Summer Olympics: A Cold War Turning Point
body: The 1980 US boycott of the Moscow Summer Olympics was a pivotal moment in the Cold War, marking a significant escalation of tensions between the United States and the Soviet Union. In response to the Soviet Union's invasion of Afghanistan in December 1979, US President Jimmy Carter announced on January 20, 1980, that the United States would boycott the upcoming Summer Olympics in Moscow.

The boycott was met with mixed reactions from other countries, with some, such as Canada, West Germany, and Japan, joining the US in boycotting the games, while others, like the United Kingdom, France, and Australia, chose to participate. The boycott ultimately involved a total of 65 countries, although some athletes from these countries did participate under the Olympic flag or as individuals.

The boycott had significant consequences for the Olympic movement, as it led to a decline in participation and a loss of revenue for the International Olympic Committee (IOC). It also marked a turning point in the Cold War, as it signaled a shift towards increased tensions and competition between the US and the Soviet Union. The boycott was widely seen as a failure, as it did not achieve its intended goal of forcing the Soviet Union to withdraw from Afghanistan, and it ultimately led to a strengthening of Soviet resolve.

The legacy of the 1980 boycott can still be seen today, as it marked a significant moment in the politicization of the Olympic Games. The boycott set a precedent for future Olympic boycotts, including the Soviet-led boycott of the 1984 Los Angeles Olympics, and it highlighted the challenges of separating sports from politics on the international stage.
cards.why: The 1980 US boycott of the Moscow Summer Olympics is significant because it highlights the complex and often fraught relationship between sports and politics on the international stage. The boycott demonstrates how global events like the Olympics can be used as a platform for political posturing and how they can be affected by broader geopolitical tensions. This has important implications for international relations, global governance, and the role of sports in shaping cultural and political discourse.

cards.title: Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks for Time-Series Forecasting
body: Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks (T-GCNs) are a type of neural network designed to handle time-series forecasting tasks on graph-structured data. They extend traditional Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) by incorporating temporal convolutional layers to model the dynamic relationships between nodes in a graph over time.

T-GCNs consist of two main components: a graph convolutional layer and a temporal convolutional layer. The graph convolutional layer uses a graph Laplacian to aggregate information from neighboring nodes, while the temporal convolutional layer uses a convolutional neural network to model the temporal relationships between nodes. This allows T-GCNs to capture both spatial and temporal dependencies in the data.

The architecture of T-GCNs can be implemented using popular deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow. For example, in PyTorch, the graph convolutional layer can be implemented using the `torch.nn` module, while the temporal convolutional layer can be implemented using the `torch.nn.functional` module. The `torch_geometric` library provides a convenient interface for working with graph-structured data in PyTorch.

T-GCNs have been applied to a variety of time-series forecasting tasks, including traffic forecasting, weather forecasting, and financial forecasting. They have been shown to outperform traditional forecasting methods, such as ARIMA and LSTM, on many of these tasks. This is because T-GCNs are able to capture the complex spatial and temporal relationships between nodes in a graph, which is not possible with traditional forecasting methods.
cards.why: Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks are significant because they enable accurate time-series forecasting in complex systems, such as traffic networks or financial markets, where traditional forecasting methods often fall short. By capturing both spatial and temporal relationships, T-GCNs can help engineers and policymakers make better decisions, such as optimizing traffic flow or predicting stock prices, which can have a major impact on people's daily lives and the economy as a whole.
